oai_dc:dc.dc:title: A Message from the President
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Waskey, Lewis “Buddy”
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: This item originally appeared in AFA Watchbird Volume 51 Number 1 (January/February 2024).The following is an excerpt from the full PDF link.President’s Message Esteemed Members and Aviculture Enthusiasts, As we navigate the early months of 2024, the landscape of U.S. Aviculture continues to evolve amidst significant challenges. In recent times, we have witnessed commendable efforts from aviculturists nationwide, diligently striving to align with the comprehensive regulations set forth by the U.S. Department of Agriculture. These regulations, pertinent to bird breeders, dealers, and exhibitors, represent a crucial step towards sustainable and responsible aviculture.The economic climate has undeniably posed substantial hurdles for all sectors, including our field. Adjusting to these changes has been imperative for continued growth and stability within our community.Looking ahead, there is a beacon of optimism as the American Federation of Aviculture (AFA) approaches a milestone - our 50th Anniversary. We are delighted to host this momentous celebration in Costa Mesa, California, a state synonymous with the origins of AFA and a pivotal hub for aviculture in the United States. The event promises to be a confluence of knowledge and celebration, featuring an impressive array of speakers, a grand banquet, and a series of both entertaining and educational activities.Click the PDF link associated with this item to view the complete item as originally published.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: AFA Watchbird; Vol. 51 No. 1 (2024): Watchbird January/February 2024; 3
